reasoning: The document exhibits severe structural failure regarding price list tables, which are the core content. While programmatic recall is high, indicating text was extracted, the fidelity report identifies that multiple vendors (Trellix, Sophos, CrowdStrike, Cisco) have tables truncated from dozens of rows down to just one or two, and four full pages of the Sophos price list are visually omitted despite remaining headers. This represents a catastrophic loss of utility for a price list document.

concerns.analysis: Several tables in the output are significantly truncated compared to the source. 1. The Trellix price list table (element index 12) is captioned as 'Lines 10-29' but only contains 2 data rows (Lines 10 and 11), missing 18 rows from that specific page range alone. 2. The CrowdStrike 'Cost Proposal Form' table (element index 17) only contains 1 row, whereas the source on page 16 shows 9 distinct cost item rows. 3. The Cisco 'Cost Proposal Form' table (element index 21) only contains 1 row, whereas

concerns.analysis: The tables for the Trellix (Formerly McAfee) section are severely misrendered and truncated. The source PDF (pages 13-15) lists 43 line items in a continuous price list. However, the HTML output splits these into separate tables that omit most of the rows. Specifically, the table for 'Lines 10-29' only displays rows 10 and 11, and the table for 'Lines 30-43' only displays row 30. Lines 1-9, 12-29, and 31-43 are missing entirely from the visual output, rendering the tables incomplete and useless

concerns.location: Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued) section.

concerns.analysis: In the Broadcom (Symantec) section, a URL in the 'Training' row of the first table is broken. The hyphen used for a line-break in the source text was included in the 'href' and 'text' properties of the link, and the final word of the URL ('courses') was excluded from the link object entirely. This results in a broken link that would not function as intended.
